mapping.a_element: generalized entropy functional built from kappa-deformed exp/log functions
kappa-deformed exponential 'canonical' distribution of generalized thermostatistics
mapping.b_element: canonical posterior distribution obtained from the updating

brief_justification: The two only share the generic theme of entropy and canonical distributions, not the driving machinery: paper A's mechanism is the algebraic construction of deformed exponential/logarithm functions satisfying prescribed criteria (generalizing the FORM of the entropy), whereas paper B's mechanism is the variational Maximum-relative-Entropy updating procedure with ordinary entropy, deriving posteriors from data and moment constraints and handling non-commuting constraints. Neither text performs the other's operation (A does no constraint-based inference/updating; B does not deform its functions), so no role-for-role correspondence of the governing process holds.
